title: Authorization, Tenant Isolation, and Abuse Resistance · Stakeholder Case: Abuse Economics
summary: Authenticated users can change identifiers, cross tenant boundaries, automate expensive actions, and exploit support or administrator workflows. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp2 abuse economics increment in deny-by-default authorization and abuse case suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Model automation, scraping, spam, enumeration, resource exhaustion, fraud, and moderator burden with proportional controls.
Propagate tenant identity through query, cache, file, job, event, log, backup, restore, and support paths with adversarial tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]

transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

steps.title: Predict Abuse Economics · Theory Abuse Economics
steps.instruction:
theory case — An authenticated member changes an identifier, crosses organization data, automates an expensive action, and exploits a standing administrator role. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Abuse Economics contract for the supplied data.

For Predict Abuse Economics · Theory Abuse Economics, keep Abuse Economics connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Abuse Economics while producing deny-by-default authorization and abuse case suite.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: An authenticated member changes an identifier, crosses organization data, automates an expensive action, and exploits a standing administrator role. The worked example variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Model automation, scraping, spam, enumeration, resource exhaustion, fraud, and moderator burden with proportional controls. A defensible solution applies Abuse Economics, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to deny-by-default authorization and abuse case suite.
